length_checks.description: Length measurement — indicates bounds checking may follow

safe_string_ops.description: Bounded string operations — prevents simple overflow but check n parameter

safe_math.description: Safe integer arithmetic — prevents integer overflow in size calculations

input_validation.description: Input parsing/validation — narrows attacker input to expected types

path_sanitizers.description: Path canonicalization — prevents traversal if combined with prefix check

memory_safety.description: Memory clearing — prevents info leaks from uninitialized buffers

access_control.description: Access control checks — validates permissions before dangerous operations

error_handling.description: Exception/signal handling — may catch exploitation attempts

kernel_field_snapshot.static_signature: Inside one function, ≥2 statements of the form `local_<X> = *(<type> *)(param_<Y> [+ <off>])` reading distinct offsets of the same source param, followed by a call to an inner function that takes `&local_<X>` (or one of the snapshotted locals' address) as the corresponding pointer argument.
kernel_field_snapshot.description: Caller-side snapshot of user-supplied struct fields onto kernel stack before forwarding to inner function. Closes any multi-fetch class TOCTOU inside the inner function because the inner sees kernel-stack copies which user threads cannot mutate from another CPU.
kernel_field_snapshot.real_world_example: CClfsLogFcbPhysical::Initialize @ clfs.sys 10.0.26100.8246 — snapshots offsets 0x00/0x08/0x10 of _CLFS_FILTER_CONTEXT into local_60/uStack_58/local_50 before calling CClfsBaseFilePersisted::CreateImage(&local_60).
kernel_field_snapshot.detected_by: scripts/multifetch_scan.py (recogniser fires when find_snapshot_callers locates the pattern in a same-tree caller)

kernel_field_constructor_store.static_signature: Somewhere in the driver, a constructor-style function does `_Dst = ExAllocatePool*(...)` (or AfdReuseEndpoint / ExpInterlockedPopEntrySList / Io/Ex/Mm/RtlAllocate*) followed by `*(<type> *)(_Dst + <off>) = scalar_value;`. The candidate function reads `*(<type> *)(param_X + <off>)` later — `param_X` is kernel-pool-allocated, the +<off> field is a scalar snapshot.
kernel_field_constructor_store.description: Value-passed-to-kernel-pool snapshot at construction time. The user buffer can be remapped/freed/rewritten after the constructor returns, but the kernel-pool struct's +<off> field holds the captured scalar permanently.
kernel_field_constructor_store.real_world_example: AfdAllocateEndpoint @ afd.sys 10.0.26100.8246 line 163 — `*(uint *)(_Dst + 0xdc) = param_6;` where param_6 was a uint VALUE read from the user IRP buffer in AfdCreate. The AFD_ENDPOINT's +0xdc field is therefore race-immune, even though AfdDelayedAcceptListen reads it three times.
kernel_field_constructor_store.detected_by: scripts/multifetch_scan.py (recogniser fires when find_constructor_stores locates a same-offset store after a kernel-pool allocator assignment, anywhere in the engagement decomp)
kernel_field_constructor_store.caveat: Fuzzy match — coincidental same-offset matches between unrelated kernel structs can produce false defenses. The scanner's composable confidence model demotes-by-1 per recogniser rather than zeroing out, so a real candidate doesn't get hidden by a single coincidental match.

file_share_exclusivity.static_signature: Kernel driver opens its private metadata files with ShareAccess = 0 (FILE_SHARE_NONE).
file_share_exclusivity.description: FS-layer defense against `CcMapData`-coherent multi-fetches. When the file backing the kernel cache page is opened with FILE_SHARE_NONE, no second user process can hold a writable handle, so the cache pages are not concurrently mutable in practice (even though `CcMapData` is theoretically coherent).
file_share_exclusivity.preflight_check: From a low-priv user shell while the kernel driver is operating: `[System.IO.File]::Open('<path>', 'Open', 'ReadWrite', 'None')`. If it returns ERROR_SHARING_VIOLATION (32), the defense is in place — mark FALSE_POSITIVE without booking kernel-debug time.
file_share_exclusivity.real_world_example: clfs.sys .blf and .bin containers — `FILE_SHARE_NONE` blocks the user-space race even though UpdateCachedOwnerPage has a textbook multi-fetch shape against `CcMapData` pages.
